triggeredBy.failure: durable target mapping missing for graph_vault/catalog when writeJsonAtomicSidecar fsyncs checksum meta parent directory during catalog books.yaml reconciliation
triggeredBy.expectedDesignQuestion: Should durable target mapping include directory fsync targets such as graph_vault/catalog and batch-run directories, or should fsyncDirectory use a parent-file mapping fallback?

r2RequiredDesignActions: complete directory fsync scopes for graph_vault settings, provider-requests, book output lancedb and shared output lancedb targets
remove the book-leases lane ownership conflict between manifestWriterLane and checkpointWriterLane
separate directoryDurableKind from primaryDurableKind in directory fsync evidence
project directory fsync evidence fields across events, command checks, status-json and recovery summary contracts
extend acceptance coverage for non-catalog directory fsync and read-only projection cases
r3RequiredDesignActions: project fsyncTarget, fsyncPlatform and fsyncErrno across command checks, status-json entries, recovery summary and item checkpoint failure evidence
add directory fsync conditional fields to subprocess durable failure projection and generic event schema
define explicit unavailable sentinels for directory fsync failures without platform errno
mark graph_vault/dspy directory fsync scope as recursive for deep DSPy policy targets
r4RequiredDesignActions: make status-json self failure projection reuse the complete requiredForFsyncBoundary field set
require sidecarKind, lane, targetMappingOwner, directoryDurableKind, primaryDurableKind and unavailableFieldSentinels in status-json self failure output for directory fsync boundaries
r1RequiredDesignActions: define directory fsync as a derived durable operation of the primary or sidecar write target
add directory fsync mapping rules for catalog, batch-run, book-scoped, dspy, output and qmd index parent directories
require directory fsync evidence fields including directoryTargetLocator, primaryTargetLocator, targetMappingOwner, lane and durableKind
state that status-json read-only and repair writer use the same directory fsync mapping without writing state in read-only mode
add acceptance coverage for catalog books.yaml checksum meta repair triggering parent directory fsync

baselineIntegrity.reason: implementation criteria were created after implementation had already been applied; no further criteria changes are allowed before this run is reconciled
baselineIntegrity.invalidatedAction: do not treat implementation-audit-r1 as a valid three-agent fixed-baseline audit
baselineIntegrity.r2InvalidatedAction: implementation-r2 criteria were also created after a partial code edit; do not use implementation-r2 as a valid fixed-baseline audit
baselineIntegrity.r3CriteriaPolicy: criteria-r3 files are frozen and must not be modified during implementation-r3 repair or audit
